name: Easy Crescent Danish Rolls (1975)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
2. In a small bowl, combine the cream cheese, sugar, and lemon juice; beat until smooth.
3. Separate the dough into 8 rectangles.
4. Firmly press the perforations to seal each rectangle.
5. Spread each rectangle with about 2 tablespoons of the cream cheese mixture.
6. Starting at the longer side, roll up each rectangle, firmly pinching the edges and ends to seal.
7. Gently stretch each roll to about 10 inches long.
8. Place each roll on an ungreased large cookie sheet, coiling each into a spiral with the seam on the inside, tucking the end under.
9. Make a deep indentation in the center of each roll; fill with about 1/2 teaspoon of preserves.
10. Bake at 350 degrees for 20 to 25 minutes, or until deeply golden brown.
11. In a small bowl, blend all glaze ingredients, adding enough milk for the desired drizzling consistency.
12. Drizzle over warm rolls.
